title: Canonical ZAR minor units and local dates
description: Define exact financial boundary and persistence representations.
decision: Expose signed monetary values as canonical two-decimal JSON strings with currency ZAR. Validate at most two fractional digits and convert once at the contract boundary to a Money value containing Int64 minor units. Persist amount_minor as SQLite INTEGER and currency_code as constrained TEXT. Persist transaction and posting dates as ISO yyyy-MM-dd TEXT; effective date is posting date when present, otherwise transaction date. Use 26-character ULID strings for durable IDs.
rationale: ZAR-only scope makes integer cents exact, sortable, and aggregatable in SQLite without decimal coercion. JSON numbers risk binary floating-point callers; decimal strings avoid that. Local bank dates are dates, not instants. ULIDs match the Lex prior art and provide stable sortable references.
alternatives: A: SQLite REAL/JSON numbers — rejected for rounding risk. B: decimal TEXT — exact but makes database aggregation and range validation harder. C: Int64 minor units with decimal-string boundary — chosen. D: full currency-scale table — deferred until non-ZAR scope exists.
consequences: Values with more than two decimals, negative zero, exponents, or Int64 overflow fail validation. The signed value always means owned-account balance delta. Presentation converts minor units back to a canonical string; no floating-point arithmetic enters actuals.
status_notes: Accepted architecture, conditionally gated: OQ-LEDGER-1, OQ-LEDGER-2, and OQ-LEDGER-3 must be resolved from all-account/both-bank evidence before Money, transaction representation, Effective Date, or dependent actuals implementation starts. Invalid evidence updates this decision and its contracts first. Planned areas: src/Tally/Domain/Ledger/Money.cs and src/Tally/Contracts/Ledger.
